name: South Carolina Early Intervention (BabyNet)
description: Part C early intervention program serving infants and toddlers birth to age 3 with developmental delays or disabilities and their families.

name: The Arc of South Carolina
description: Statewide advocacy organization for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities, providing resources, programs, and community support.

name: Special Olympics South Carolina
description: Year-round sports training and competition for children and adults with intellectual disabilities throughout South Carolina.

name: Protection and Advocacy for People with Disabilities (P&A SC)
description: Federally designated protection and advocacy organization providing legal advocacy for South Carolinians with disabilities.

name: South Carolina Vocational Rehabilitation Department
description: State vocational rehabilitation program providing employment services, job training, and placement for South Carolinians with disabilities.

name: NAMI South Carolina
description: State chapter of the National Alliance on Mental Illness providing free mental health support, educational classes, online support groups, and advocacy for individuals and families affected by mental illness.

name: South Carolina Assistive Technology Program (SCATP)
description: South Carolina's assistive technology program providing device demonstrations, equipment loans, and AT training for individuals with disabilities.

name: Palmetto ABLE Savings Account
description: Tax-advantaged ABLE savings accounts for South Carolinians with disabilities to save without affecting eligibility for public benefits.

name: MUSC Shawn Jenkins Children's Hospital - Developmental Pediatrics
description: Developmental and behavioral pediatrics at the Medical University of South Carolina providing comprehensive evaluations and treatment for children with developmental concerns.

name: Coastal Adaptive Sports
description: Nonprofit facilitating adaptive sports opportunities and recreation activities to enhance the lives of individuals and families with disabilities, including wheelchair basketball, cycling, and surfing.
